join_order.build: nation (25 rows → direct_array[25] of char[26], indexed by n_nationkey)
join_order.rationale: 25 rows, trivially small; direct array eliminates all join overhead

join_order.build: supplier (100K rows → direct_array[100001] of int32_t s_nationkey, indexed by s_suppkey)
join_order.rationale: 100K entries × 4B = 400KB; fits in L2/L3; O(1) lookup with zero hash overhead

join_order.build: part filtered by p_name LIKE '%green%' (96K rows → bitset[2M bits] + bloom_filter[96K keys])
join_order.probe: lineitem.l_partkey (bloom first, then bitset) and partsupp.ps_partkey (bitset only)
join_order.rationale: Bitset (256KB) and bloom (~120KB) both fit in L3/L2; eliminates 95.2% of lineitem rows before any hash lookup

join_order.build: partsupp filtered (ps_partkey in part_bitset → ~384K rows → compact_hash_map(uint64 composite_key → double ps_supplycost), ~12MB, fits in L3)
join_order.probe: lineitem qualifying rows via composite(l_partkey, l_suppkey) key
join_order.rationale: Building on filtered side (384K not 8M); 12MB hash map is L3-resident for all 2.88M probes → cache hits

join_order.build: orders (15M rows → partitioned_compact_hash_map(int32 o_orderkey → int32 o_orderdate), 16 partitions × ~938K entries × 8B = ~15MB per partition)
join_order.rationale: Partitioned build enables parallel construction; per-partition table fits in L3; avoids serial 15M-insert bottleneck

join_order.build: none (lineitem is always the probe/fact side)
join_order.probe: lineitem (60M rows scanned; bloom pre-filter reduces to ~2.88M qualifying rows for all subsequent joins)
join_order.rationale: Star schema rule: never build on fact table; lineitem is always probed last

logical_plan.aggregation_note: GROUP BY (n_name, o_year): 25 nations × 7 years (1992-1998) = 175 max groups; use double agg[25][7] thread-local, index by [nation_idx=s_nationkey][o_year-1992]; all fits in L1 cache (175 × 8B = 1.4KB per thread)

filter_pushdown.applied_at: sequential scan of part/p_name.bin before any join

part_filter_bitset.set_on: qualifying p_partkey values (96K bits set out of 2M)

part_bloom_filter.purpose: Pre-filter lineitem.l_partkey before the more expensive bitset+hash probes; eliminates ~95.2% of 60M lineitem rows at L2 speed

partsupp_composite_map.build_from: 8M partsupp rows filtered to ~384K via part_bitset

aggregation_buffer.merge_strategy: sequential reduction of 64 thread-local arrays into single global array (64 × 1.4KB = 89.6KB total)

build_sequence.action: Fill n_name_arr[n_nationkey[i]] = n_name[i] for i in 0..24
Fill s_nationkey_arr[s_suppkey[i]] = s_nationkey[i] for i in 0..99999
Parallel scan; for each row: strstr(p_name_ptr + i*56, 'green'); on match: set part_bitset[p_partkey] and bloom.insert(hash(p_partkey)). Thread-local bitsets; OR-merge after scan.

Parallel scan 8M rows; check ps_partkey in part_bitset; collect ~384K qualifying (composite_key, ps_supplycost) pairs into thread-local buffers; merge-insert into single compact hash map (small enough for sequential build of 384K entries at ~3.84ms)

64 threads scan orders in morsels; each thread routes (o_orderkey, o_orderdate) pairs to 16 partition buffers using (o_orderkey & 15); after scan, 16 threads build one partition hash table each (937K inserts each, ~10ms per partition in parallel)
build_sequence.threading: partition_parallel(16 partitions, 64 threads scan + 16 threads build)

filter_and_join_sequence.cost_note: bloom is L2-resident (~120KB); ~1ns per row; 60M × 1ns = 60ms / 64 threads ≈ 1ms
bitset is L3-resident (256KB); ~5ns per surviving bloom candidate
12MB map is L3-resident; ~10ns per probe; 2.88M × 10ns ≈ 29ms / 64 threads < 1ms

filter_and_join_sequence.predicate: partition = l_orderkey & 15; probe partition_map[partition] for l_orderkey → o_orderdate
filter_and_join_sequence.cost_note: 16MB per partition; likely L3-cached; ~10-20ns per probe

filter_and_join_sequence.predicate: n_name = n_name_arr[nationkey] (for GROUP BY key; use nationkey as group index directly)

filter_and_join_sequence.method: linear scan of year_starts[8] = {8035,8401,8766,9131,9496,9862,10227,10592}; o_year = 1992 + y where year_starts[y] <= o_orderdate < year_starts[y+1]

filter_and_join_sequence.cost_note: 2 FMA operations; ~1-2ns per row with FP pipeline
L1-resident 1.4KB array; ~1ns per update; zero synchronization

phase_details.part_scan: 64 threads; thread-local bitset words (OR-merged after); bloom shared (atomic OR on words during build, or built sequentially from merged bitset)
phase_details.partsupp_scan: 64 threads collect matching rows into thread-local vectors; single thread builds hash map from merged 384K rows
phase_details.orders_build: 64 threads route rows to 16 partition buffers (one mutex per partition or lock-free append); 16 threads build partition hash tables in parallel
phase_details.lineitem_probe: 64 threads, each owns independent agg[25][7]; no shared state during probe; sequential merge of 64 arrays (89.6KB total) takes <1ms

index_usage.purpose: NOT USED — part scan is done sequentially anyway for p_name substring filter; partkey bitset is built directly from the scan; hash index not needed
OPTIONAL FALLBACK — mmap this 288MB persistent index if in-memory partsupp hash map build is skipped; preferred plan builds a smaller 12MB in-memory map from 384K filtered rows instead
index_usage.decision: skip (in-memory build preferred: 12MB vs 288MB, better cache behavior)

index_usage.purpose: NOT USED — persistent index is 402MB (33M slots × 12B); building a 16-partition in-memory map (total 240MB) enables better cache locality per partition (16MB each fits in L3)
NOT USED — replaced by direct_array[100001] indexed by s_suppkey; O(1) without hash computation

physical_plan.scan_strategy_note: All predicates and join probes are fused into the lineitem scan loop; no materialization of intermediate row sets; single sequential pass through all 6 lineitem column files
memory_layout_notes: Lineitem columns scanned sequentially in parallel (good prefetcher behavior)
Bloom filter (120KB): permanently L2-resident across all 64 threads
Bitset (256KB) + partsupp map (12MB): L3-resident, shared read-only across threads
Orders partitioned map (16MB/partition): each partition L3-resident when actively probed
Thread-local agg arrays (1.4KB each): permanently L1-resident

year_extraction.group_index: o_year - 1992 → index 0..6 into agg second dimension

output_phase.groups: up to 175 (25 × 7); in practice all 175 likely populated
output_phase.sort: std::sort on flat array of {nation_name, o_year, sum_profit} structs; 175 elements; ORDER BY nation ASC, o_year DESC

estimated_cost.confidence: medium-high: dominant cost is 2.16GB lineitem scan across 64 threads; bloom pre-filter reduces expensive hash probes to 2.88M rows; all critical hash maps are L3-resident
